entry.title: AUoM-Based Warehouse Task Splitting in SAP EWM?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working on a requirement in SAP EWM where we need to split warehouse tasks (WTs) based on the alternate unit of measure (AUoM) defined in the product master. The goal is to optimize picking by creating WTs in the largest possible UoM first — for example, Pallet > Case > Each — and then cascade down based on available stock and delivery quantity.

Here’s a simple example of the AUoM setup in product master:

  • 1 Case = 10 EA
  • 1 Pallet = 100 EA

If the delivery quantity is 312 EA, the system should ideally create:

  • 1 WT for 3 Pallets (300 EA)
  • 1 WT for 1 Case (10 EA)
  • 1 WT for 2 Each (2 EA)

Let me know your thoughts on effectively achieving this

entry.title: SAP TM public cloud manually change between FTL and LTL
entry.content:
Hi Community,

In our S/4HANA Public Cloud tenant we use Transportation Management.
When I create a Road Freight Order I only get one document type – SFO2 (Road Freight Order) – and it is hard-wired to Shipping Type 18 (FTL).
The Shipping Type field itself is read-only, and the usual SSCUI “Define Freight Order Types” (103983) does not exist in Public Cloud.

Questions

  1. What is the supported way to make Shipping Type 17 (LTL) available?

  2. Do we have to raise a Request Expert Configuration so SAP copies SFO2 and sets Shipping Type 17, or is there any self-service alternative?

  3. Could condition-based FO-type determination switch between FTL and LTL at planning/tender time?

  4. Any best-practice tips from projects that already solved this?

(See screenshot below – Shipping Type is locked on FTL.)

 

The ultimate situation I would like is if I can manually choose between FTL or LTL. 

Thanks for any insights!

Best regards,
Floris

entry.title: Re: The PR/PO does not commit the budget
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

First of all, just to clarify — we’re talking about S/4HANA Public Cloud, not the Private Edition. The Public version handles budgeting differently and has already evolved. 😄

That said, I believe this might be a program error. I recommend opening an incident with SAP, as this is a relatively new feature and may still have some issues. I have access to a current version and have already noticed some problems with commitments.

Given that, I don't think the issue is related to the Availability Control (AVC) itself, but rather to how commitments are tagged semantically. You can test this by opening the “Project Costs Report” app — check if the commitment appears there. Most likely, it won’t. The same behavior probably happens in the “Project Budget Report” app.

In my opinion, this is the root cause: there’s a logic error in the Public Cloud version that fails to properly determine commitments.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: APM Role uses
entry.content:
What permissions do each of the following roles give a user?

AdminFlexKeyUser

FlexKeyUser

FlexPublicViewEditor

KeyUser

MeasurementDocument_Create

MeasurementDocument_Read

MeasurementDocument_Sync

RuleRepositorySuperUser

RuleRuntimeSuperUser  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: APM Role uses
entry.content:
Roles AdminFlexKeyUser, FlexKeyUser, FlexPublicViewEditor, KeyUser are for creating key user extensions.

This link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_APM/f3dde866a96d4638bd2ac2f18ea83cbc/025f7a4eb0f74126b09c0651ac9c4b9e.html doesn't go into an what each role does specifically, but what the roles in general allow a user to do.

 

Roles MeasurementDocument_Create, MeasurementDocument_Read & MeasurementDocument_Sync are for certain rules API's. These API's are not public API's but the system does use them. You won't find them on the accelerator hub.

 

Role RuleRepositorySuperUser gives you:

  • Access to the Manage Rule Projects application and repository APIs to model and manage rules.

  • The permission to grant access to a business rules project for other users using privilege APIs. Go to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and choose Privileges on the left menu.

Role RuleRuntimeSuperUser gives you access to deploy and execute the ruleservices. 

For more rule roles please see: https://help.sap.com/docs/business-rules/business-rules-capability-for-cloud-foundry-environment/authorization-configuration

entry.title: Issue CDN on Workzone Lauchpad with Referenced Fiori App - deploy on Cloud foundry

"dependencies": {  "minUI5Version": "1.114.0",
"libs": {  "sap.fin.gl.lib.posting": {

},

But still going to wrong place.

( its a mandatory requirement to have this app on BTP, We can not deploy it on backend )

@Wouter @FabioPagoti  any ideias? thankks

entry.title: Archiving not possible for application V6: Object type is missing
entry.content:
We have a requirement to archive Handling Unit into ArchiveLink server. We have successfully configured the same in systems and shipment document when saved, output is triggered and archiving is being done. But for associated HU, we are unable to issue output with medium- External Send and Storage mode- Print or Print & Archive. Any solution on this please?

System throws error when issuing output against HU from VT02N or from VL74 that 

Archiving not possible for application V6: Object type is missing

Message no. VN049

Diagnosis

Optical archiving requires an object type. You can define the object type per output application in Table 681Z.

Procedure

You must include a valid object type for the output application in Table 681Z.

 

It seems no entry is available in T681Z table against V6 application along with Hdtable- KOMKBV6. Where object type- BUS3070 must be available. Like for V7 we have VTTK object type is available in this same table and no issue is there. 

 

What is the configuration path or solution to make an entry against BUS3070 object for Handling unit over here?

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields: How to add a custom field to a specific Fiori UI section
entry.content:
Have you ever created a key user custom field and wanted to display it in a standard SAP Fiori UI, but didn't know which OData service entity to enable to make the field available in the field list offered by Fiori Runtime Adaptation?

If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you. It explains how you can find out the respective OData service entity manually (called Variant 1: Manual way). It also explains a much simpler alternative how to enable the OData service entity for the given field using the integration between Fiori Runtime Adaptation and the key user custom field tool (called Variant 2: Automatic way).

Variant 1: Manual way

The first step is to identify which OData service entity provides the data for the UI section in the Fiori UI of the SAP standard application. Once you have this information, you can enable the custom field in the Custom Fields app for the specified OData service entity. This action makes the custom field available in the list of fields within Fiori Runtime Adaptation.

Let’s assume that you created a custom field named “Priority” on the business context SD_SALESDOC, and you want to expose this custom field in the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 (Fiori app ID F3893). You want to place the field in the “Basic Data” section of the “General Information” section of the Sales Order UI (the location is marked with a red cross within the screenshot).

You want to place the custom field here

First, you need to find out which OData service entity provides the data for the UI section where you want to place the field.
You can find this out by adhering to the following steps:

  1. Open the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 and navigate to the respective screen within this UI (in our case, it’s the detail screen of the sales order)
  2. Use the key combination CTRL + SHIFT + ALT + S. A Fiori Diagnostics dialog pops up
  3. Within the dialog UI, open the Control Tree section
  4. Go back to the “General Information Basic Data” section of the Sales Order UI and press CTRL + ALT + SHIFT. Then, pick the UI section or field you are interested in within the application UI. In our example, we used the “Order Reason” field (make sure that you select the field and not its label). The control in the control tree representing this field is selected
  5. Now, you can select the “Binding Info” tab shown on the right side of the control tree screen. In the “path” field, you’ll see the name “SalesOrderManage” of the OData entity where the field is bound against (see subsequent screenshot).

Find the OData entity set name within the binding information for a field

Equipped with this information, you can now navigate back to the custom fields tool. Go to the details screen of your custom field (in our example, it’s the field “YY1_Priority”) and open the “User Interfaces” tab. Enter “SalesOrderManage” in the search bar.
This leads to the following result:

Find the OData entity set name within the binding information for a field

In the “Data Source” column, you’ll now see the “SalesOrderManageType” data source. This corresponds to the entity set name “SalesOrderManage”. The different suffix “Type” is used because in the Custom Fields app, the entity type is shown instead of the entity set name.

There may be cases where several OData services share the same OData entity set name, and all of them support exposing the respective custom fields. In this case, you can find out which of the search results is the right one by comparing the description of the OData service entry in the custom fields tool with the info you see in the “Inherited from” field within the Binding Infos tab of the control tree. Since only the technical name and not the description is shown, it won’t match exactly, but in a lot of cases it’s quite similar.

Alternatively, you can compare the names in the Custom Fields app with the ones contained in the Configuration section within Implementation Information tab of the relevant Fiori app in SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library:

OData service(s) related to Fiori app

Clicking “Enable Usage” and publishing the field makes it available for the respective UI section using Runtime Adaptation.

Variant 2: Automatic way

Please note that you can also use the integration between UI Runtime Adaptation and the custom fields tool to make this process easier:

  1. Open the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 and navigate to the respective screen within this UI (in our case, it’s the detail screen of the sales order)
  2. Switch into Runtime Adaptation mode by selecting the “Adapt UI” option in the drop-down menu. You can get to the drop-down menu by clicking on your avatar on the top right icon representing your user
  3. Select the field or section you’re interested in. Right-click to select “+ Add: Field” from the drop-down menu
  4. A popup displays all available fields and business contexts. Mostly, there is only one – in our case it’s “Sales: Sales Document”. A “Create” option on the right top of the popup allows you to select the “Custom Field” sub-option. Clicking “Create” you’ll be navigated to the Custom Fields app. The Custom Fields app now knows from which UI section you’ve navigated to it. It also understands which OData service entity was bound against the UI section from where you came from. The business context is also taken as pre-selection information.
  5. If you create a new custom field, then the respective OData service entity is enabled automatically. If you navigate to the details of an already existing field, you’ll get a popup asking whether you want to enable the respective OData service entity you are coming from. When you select “Yes”, this will be done automatically for you (see screenshot below):

Popup allowing you to “automatically” enable the respective OData service entity for the given custom field

In case you select “No”, when you navigate back to the “User Interfaces” tab, the mentioned OData service and the OData entity will be marked with a blue bar to make it easier for you to find (see screenshot below).

OData service and OData entity marked in the Custom Fields app

Now, you’re done, and you’ve found out which OData service entity to enable to make the field available in the field list offered by Fiori Runtime Adaptation.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide them in the comments section below. Also, please follow my profile to get updates when I publish more posts on these topics.
Also feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

I want to thank Yasmina van Rooij  and Karsten Schaser for their extremely helpful contributions.

Other related blog posts and other infos:

  • https://blogs.sap.com/2022/06/23/easily-manage-your-key-user-adaptations-with-sapui5-flexibility/
  • SAP Help - Adapt UI
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-screen-personas-how-to-position-key-user-custom-fields-exactly-where/ba-p/13553723

entry.title: Cannot create variant with UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service and Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I want to enable the variant management for a Fiori List Report running standalone using the UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service.
I followed the documentation for using the service with standalone applications (https://help.sap.com/docs/ui5-flexibility-for-key-users/ui5-flexibility-for-key-users/developing-applications-running-standalone?locale=en-US)
I added the flexibility service to the bootstrap and the route to the xs-app.json.

The /settings and /data requests work fine, but when I try to save a variant the service returns HTTP 500

This is the request body for the POST request /keyuser/flex/all/v3/changes?sap-language=en

[{"reference":"<appname>","namespace":"apps/<appname>/variants/","projectId":"<appname>","support":{"generator":"FlexObjectFactory.createFlVariant","user":"<username>","sapui5Version":"1.124.11"},"originalLanguage":"EN","layer":"USER","fileType":"ctrl_variant","fileName":"id_1751547421622_174_flVariant","content":{},"texts":{"variantName":{"value":"test","type":"XFLD"}},"favorite":true,"executeOnSelection":false,"contexts":{},"variantReference":"<appname>::<entity>--fe::PageVariantManagement","variantManagementReference":"<appname>::<entity>--fe::PageVariantManagement"},{"changeType":"addCondition","reference":"<appname>","namespace":"apps/<appname>/changes/","projectId":"<appname>","packageName":"$TMP","support":{"generator":"FlexObjectFactory.createFromFileContent","user":"<username>","sourceChangeFileName":"id_1751547411208_167_addCondition","sapui5Version":"1.124.11"},"originalLanguage":"EN","layer":"USER","fileType":"change","fileName":"id_1751547421623_175_addCondition","content":{"name":"createdAt","condition":{"operator":"TODAY","values":[],"validated":"NotValidated"}},"texts":{},"selector":{"id":"<appname>::<entity>--fe::FilterBar::<entity>","idIsLocal":false},"dependentSelector":{},"variantReference":"id_1751547421622_174_flVariant"}]

There is no additional information in the response, I also did not find any further information in the logs of the approuter or the audit logs.

I tried with the SAPUI5 version 1.124.9 and 1.136.1, both are not working. My user has assigned all roles provided by the service.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to delete fs item
entry.content:
Hi @ArnoldWarhonowicz ,

May be try couple of things

A. Ensure the FS item is not in use with posted data
Even after removing it from the global settings, you cannot delete the FS item if:

It has posted data (actuals or plan)
It is used in consolidation group transactions
It is referenced in elimination entries or journal entries

Use the Group Data Analysis or Group Journal Entries apps to verify if the FS item was used in past postings.


B. Delete or deactivate
Once it's no longer used in settings or transactions:

Go to the Define FS Items app
Locate the FS item
Delete or set the status to inactive (if deletion is not permitted)

Last try:-

SSCUI 102639 – Define Consolidation of Investments Settings
Accessible via Manage Your Solution > Configure Your Solution
Look for investment-related mappings that include your FS item.Make a change if required.

I cannot think of any other possibiity apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.


Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Monitoring Non-SAP Application Via Cloud ALM
entry.content:
One of SAP’s core strategic goals is to empower IT teams by simplifying complexity and seamlessly integrating diverse systems, whether they’re SAP or not. In today's enterprise environments, organizations rely on a wide array of tools, platforms, and custom applications. Recognizing this, SAP aims to provide a centralized, unified platform where all of these components, cloud-native services, third-party systems can be monitored, managed, and optimized from one place: Cloud ALM.

This is a powerful cloud-native solution built to manage the entire lifecycle of both SAP and non-SAP applications, from initial deployment to ongoing operations. With its real-time insights and proactive capabilities, it gives you a holistic view of your IT landscape, helping ensure system health, detect issues early, and maintain governance regardless of where your workloads are running or how your architecture is structured.

What does this blog covers?

In this post, we’ll dive into how SAP Cloud ALM can integrate with non‑SAP applications, and review the available scenarios and methods to connect external systems and fully leverage its monitoring capabilities.

While SAP Cloud ALM already offers out-of-the-box (plug-and-play) integrations with well-known platforms like Microsoft Teams, Jira, ServiceNow, Tricentis, Signavio, and WalkMe, it also provides flexible integration paths for any non‑SAP solution. Let’s explore some key facts:

Cloud ALM Integration Key Facts for Non‑SAP Applications:

1. Open Platform Architecture
SAP Cloud ALM is built on an open architecture and can monitor any non‑SAP application capable of sending telemetry data, such as metrics, logs, and traces, using OpenTelemetry Protocol.

2. Support for Custom Apps on SAP BTP
It can seamlessly monitor custom applications developed in SAP BTP, whether on Cloud Foundry or Kyma runtime, using Java or Node.js.

3. Support multiple monitoring modules in Cloud ALM
Integration & Exception Monitoring is the primary module for ingesting data (logs, metrics, traces) from non‑SAP sources.
Health Monitoring can also be used for custom applications running in SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, particularly for Java and Node.js services.

4. Native Support for OpenTelemetry
Any application, SAP or not, that supports OpenTelemetry (OTLP protocol) can send telemetry to Cloud ALM using the Raw Data Inbound API. This also allows integration with modern, cloud-native platforms which are not hosted in SAP BTP environments.

In summary:

Due to the fact these are all external (non‑SAP) systems, there are no pre-built native connectors. Instead, integration is performed via robust and secure APIs offered by SAP Cloud ALM:

  • Inbound API: for sending structured events and exception messages
  • Raw Data Inbound API (OpenTelemetry): for sending telemetry data such as metrics, logs, and traces
  • Analytics, Landscape & Event APIs: for reading system data and enabling external dashboards or alerts

API integration is the standard approach. There are no native connectors for non-SAP platforms.

How to get started

Here are the official guides to help you implement any scenario described above:

Adding Non-SAP Services and Systems | SAP Help Portal

OpenTelemetry@SAP

SAP Cloud ALM APIs for Operations

Integration & Exception Monitoring - Setup & Configuration

SAP ALM API & Integration: Open Telemetry Support ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello all, 

We are facing a challenge with "Schedule Contract Settlement - Customer Contracts" app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. 

We are looking to schedule the Delta Accruals settlement to run recurringly (daily), considering only the period up to the execution date. 

However, we've encountered the following behavior: 

      - The app requires a fixed settlement date.

      - If a broad date range is entered (e.g 01.01 - 31.12), the system processes the entire period at once.

      - There does not appear to be an option to use a dynamic date

We would like to know if there is any official or recommended approach to schedule Delta Accruals with a dynamic settlement date (e.g "today"). 

Thank you in advance for any guidance.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: What is the GS1-128 barcode format
entry.content: Hello @MahmoudElshall, I don't how you can manage it with serial number, but all what I know is that the GTIN number in the barcode should be on 14 characters. No less, no more. There is no separator ]C1 after the GTIN, so the system is not able to know where it stop if it's not on 14 characters. Regards Emmanuel

entry.title: Re: Migration Template not available for App " Manage Ren...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

There is an SAP Help Portal article available which lists out all available migration object in a searchable, filterable way. You can immediately click through the migration objects to get access to their specific documentations and prerequisites as well.

And in case you cannot find a migration object listed as available, you can follow the process outlined in KBA 2676589 to learn more about how you can submit feature requests related to Data Migration activities.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Ana_Figueira, 

I would recommend to use a Factory calendar, which is set up with daily dates and answering your requirements of execution, for the Delta accruals in the condition contract. When generating the calendar using factory calendar, the system will create every single Settlement date accordingly. 

You can then execute the Settlement via the corresponding tile, and set up a job with a variant created with dynamic date selection for the settlement date if required. 

You also have an option to use the Unplanned Settlement tile available after 2023 version I believe. This tile is pretty much the same as the first one but will create the Settlement date entry in the contract if it doesn't exist. 

Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: how to compare security roles between users
entry.content:
Hi @KRahilly 

You're right SUIM is a sort of manual comparison.

The best way which i do for my client is

1. Use Table AGR_USERS for direct comparison

Go to SE16N, Enter table: AGR_USERS, Filter for UNAME = 'USER1' and download, Repeat for USER2.
Compare the list in Excel.

2. Run report: RSUSR070 — Display Comparison Between Users

Run RSUSR070, Enter both User IDs, Choose what to compare (roles, profiles, authorizations), Execute.
You’ll get a side-by-side list of roles, with flags showing what is common and what differs.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP, HDI and Datasphere
entry.content: You can also reference the Datasphere HANA DB as the storage for your CAP app. It's not as straightforward as the prior answer, but it allows you to have local data for your Datasphere views and CAP apps.

entry.title: Re: Remove failed requests in Offline Mobile Development...
entry.content:
Hi @akhthaaralibadhusha,

Yes this is possible. For all failed requests during offline synchronization, a new entity will be added in the ErrorArchive entity set. If you execute a delete action on that specific ErrorArchive entity, the request which fails will be removed from the list of pending requests for the next offline synchronization. 

This is documented with sample implementation for our native SDKs, but is also applicable for MDK applications: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/offline/common/handling-failed-requests/reverting-error-state.html#reverting-error-state

entry.title: Re: Enable Custom field in app Create Financial Transacti...
entry.content:
Hi @david-nguyen ,

Adapt UI allows visual placement of the field,however, It does not persist the data unless it's bound to a Business Context
Even if the field appears, it's not stored or transferred to the accounting document.

Check for extensibility support
Go to the app: Custom Fields and Logic (F1481)
Search for Business Contexts like:
Accounting: Journal Entry Item or Accounting: Journal Entry or Cash Journal Entry Item (if available)
If none are linked to Create Financial Transactions, you can’t bind custom fields via this route.

Try workaround

1. Use Journal Entry Extension via BADI (if available)
AC_DOCUMENT_ENHANCE – Enhance Accounting Document
BADI_FINS_ACDOC_HDR_CHG – Change Header Data
BADI_FINS_ACDOC_ITEM_CHG – Change Item Data

2. Switch to Using Journal Entry APIs or the Post General Journal Entries App

If the business process allows, consider using:
App: Post General Journal Entries (F0718)
This app does support extensions via business context: Accounting: Journal Entry Item

Steps:
Go to Custom Fields and Logic
Choose business context: Accounting: Journal Entry Item
Create your field
Enable it in:
-UIs: Post General Journal Entries
-CDS Views: I_JournalEntryItemCube, etc.

I cannot think of any other possibility apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: MRP to create purchase requisition : BADI MD_PURREQ_CHANGE to add G/L Account & Costcenter
entry.content:
Hi ABAP gurus, 

The requirement is change the account assignment category and add G/L Account & Cost center on the new purchase requisition created by triggering MRP ( MD03 ) .

I am able to change the change the Account Assignment Category ( KNTTP ) through the BADI implementation of MD_PURREQ_CHANGE in the Method CHANGE_BEFORE_SAVE_MRP  using  ch_eban-knttp  BUT in the same method the fields G/L Account ( ch_ebkn-sakto )  & Cost center ( ch_ebkn-kostl ) are also there for which i am passing the desired valid values.

However, G/L Account & Cost center are not getting updated. I went through the sap reference note for this SAP Note Number : 1824615 and am using CH_CHANGED = 'X' and  account assignment category (  ch_eban-knttp ) is successfully getting updated. 

BADI ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST METHOD PROCESS_ACCOUNT will not get triggered here because ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST  is designed specifically for dialog transactions (ME51N, ME52N) where a user is interacting with the screen. MRP is a background process that does not use the same screen logic, so it doesn't call  ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST BAdI.

Sample code screenshot below :

How can G/L Account & Cost center be added to the new  purchase requisition creation process using triggering of MRP ( MD03 )  using SAP ABAP ?  

Would be grateful to your replies. 

Thanks,

Devendra

entry.title: Re: Transfer historical unbilled revenue to Enteprise pro...
entry.content:
Hello @Nikhil1905 

Thank you for the clarification. 

Unbilled revenue reflected in Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) can only be generated through project-integrated transactions, such as below

  • Time confirmations
  • Procurement or service entry postings
  • Billing plan execution

Manual General Journal Entries, even with project assignments, will not trigger unbilled revenue calculations within EBRR.

For historical unbilled revenue

You can migrate balances at project/WBS level using the Data Migration Cockpit for reporting purposes

However, these migrated figures do not interact with EBRR logic — only integrated project postings can influence revenue recognition

Cloud you let me know if you are working with Fixed Price or Time & Material projects, or specifically Cost-Based POC? (You mentioned Cost-Based POC, just want to confirm the context)

Please refer to SAP Help – Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Projects for Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Review and Adapt Business Roles after a Major Upgrade in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi,

You asked a good question. One important thing keeps in mind is that all changes are made in D, then transport to T and P tenants.

Before upgrade, you review those upcoming changes from SAP's documentation and make a potential change list. After T tenant is upgrade, you can verify these changes in an upgraded T system. Even you confirm these changes in T tenant, you don't make changes there. Now you have a verified list of changes to be made. Four weeks later, over the upgrade weekend, you make changes in the upgraded D tenant, then move the transports to T and test it in T. When the test is successful, then you move the transport to P tenant.

In short, you don' t make changes in T when it is upgraded.  Changes will be made four weeks later during D/P upgrade weekend. 

Hope this answers your questions.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Splitstorm - A Generic Splitter Configuration Tool
entry.content:
Extracting and Restoring Splitter Container Configurations in SAP GUI with ABAP

When working with complex SAP GUI applications, it's common to use CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER to structure screens. These splitter configurations often evolve over time and differ between users, environments, or sSAPGUI-settings.

This article presents a simple but effective way to extract and later restore the current configuration of SAP splitter containers using a small local ABAP utility that I call SplitStorm.

The core idea is not product-related. It's about treating layout configurations as data that can be stored and restored. While SAP provides APIs to define and modify split containers, it lacks built-in support for introspection and persistence of these layout states.

Problem Statement

There are a few typical situations where this becomes relevant:

  • Users have different SAPGUI settings (theme, fonts or fontsize) which results in different output and hidden information. 

  • Different users have different interest on different areas. One user needs to have a bigger view on the global ALV-Grid, another colleague wants to see more of the detail view.
  • Different screen resolutions result in unused screen areas, e.g. when the ratio is set to 30% for the left object tree, what fits great on FullHD but is to much for higher resolutions or wide screens

Approach

Using recursive traversal and some clever techniques, it’s possible to extract the layout structure and size ratios of a splitter hierarchy. The data is stored in a simple, format that can later be used to reconstruct the exact same configuration.

The main features include:

  • Reading the layout hierarchy of nested CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER instances

  • Capturing the orientation, pane sizes, and nesting relationships

  • Rebuilding the structure with the same layout at a later time

How does it work?

The method ANALYZE_AND_SAVE analyzes the container hierarchy of the given main container. To retrieve the number of rows and columns of a splitter, I simply try to get all containers in a 20x20-Grid. If I do not get a valid container for row 3 and column 4 then I know that there are max 2 rows or max 3 columns.

The height and width for each pane will be read and stored in a simple matrix information

Data will be saved in the Index-Table INDX where data in any format can be stored easily. A better way would be to transform the data to JSON or serialize in a data object and store it in an own table. But for my purposes I think that INDX is okay.

Restoring the data reads the path to the current container and sets height and width. 

It's easy to build the standard layout and the restore the configuration from saved data.

The Code

The actual complete source code is available at github: Splitstorm

Demo report

The Demo report builds a sample configuration using splitter containers without any controls in it.

You can save the current configuration, load or delete an existing layout.

REPORT zt9r_splitstorm_demo01.

SELECTION-SCREEN PUSHBUTTON /1(20) TEXT-sav USER-COMMAND zsave.
SELECTION-SCREEN PUSHBUTTON /1(20) TEXT-lod USER-COMMAND zload.
SELECTION-SCREEN PUSHBUTTON /1(20) TEXT-del USER-COMMAND zdelete.

INITIALIZATION.

  DATA(docker) = NEW cl_gui_docking_container( ratio = 90 side = cl_gui_docking_container=>dock_at_bottom ).

  DATA(sca)   = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = docker rows = 2 columns = 2 ).
  DATA(sca1)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca->get_container( row = 1 column = 1 ) rows = 1 columns = 2 ).
  DATA(sca2)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca->get_container( row = 2 column = 1 ) rows = 2 columns = 1 ).
  DATA(scb1)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca2->get_container( row = 1 column = 1 ) rows = 1 columns = 2 ).
  DATA(scb2)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca2->get_container( row = 2 column = 1 ) rows = 2 columns = 1 ).


AT SELECTION-SCREEN.

  DATA(info) = NEW zt9r_splitstorm( sy-cprog ).

  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'ZSAVE'.
      info->analyze_and_save( docker ).
    WHEN 'ZLOAD'.
      info->load_and_restore( docker ).
    WHEN 'ZDELETE'.
      info->delete( ).
  ENDCASE.

Main class

CLASS zt9r_splitstorm DEFINITION PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_splitter_info,
             path          TYPE string,
             rows          TYPE i,
             columns       TYPE i,
             row_heights   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY,
             column_widths TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY,
           END OF ty_splitter_info.

    TYPES ty_splitter_info_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_splitter_info WITH DEFAULT KEY.

    METHODS delete.

    METHODS load_and_restore
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.

    METHODS analyze_and_save
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.

    METHODS constructor
      IMPORTING
        repid TYPE clike.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS save.
    METHODS load.

    CONSTANTS mc_root TYPE string VALUE `ROOT`.

    METHODS analyze_recursive
      IMPORTING
        i_container   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_control
        i_path        TYPE string
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE abap_bool.

    METHODS get_row_count
      IMPORTING
        i_splitter    TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE i.

    METHODS get_column_count
      IMPORTING
        i_splitter    TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE i.

    METHODS restore_recursive
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_control
        i_path      TYPE string.

    METHODS get_splitter_row_height
      IMPORTING
        io_splitter   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(r_size) TYPE i.

    METHODS get_splitter_col_width
      IMPORTING
        io_splitter   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(r_size) TYPE i.

    METHODS analyze
      IMPORTING
        i_container   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE ty_splitter_info_tab.

    METHODS restore_sizes
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.

    METHODS get_id
      RETURNING
        VALUE(r_id) TYPE char20.

    DATA size_info TYPE ty_splitter_info_tab.
    DATA repid     TYPE c LENGTH 40.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZT9R_SPLITSTORM IMPLEMENTATION.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->ANALYZE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        TY_SPLITTER_INFO_TAB
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD analyze.
    LOOP AT i_container->children INTO DATA(child).
      analyze_recursive( i_container = child
                         i_path      = mc_root ).
    ENDLOOP.

    result = size_info.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->ANALYZE_AND_SAVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD analyze_and_save.
    analyze( i_container = i_container ).
    save( ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->ANALYZE_RECURSIVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTROL
* | [--->] I_PATH                         TYPE        STRING
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        ABAP_BOOL
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD analyze_recursive.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_splitter) = CAST cl_gui_splitter_container( i_container ).
        result = abap_true.
      CATCH cx_sy_move_cast_error.
        result = abap_false.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

    DATA(lv_rows)    = get_row_count( lo_splitter ).
    DATA(lv_columns) = get_column_count( lo_splitter ).

    DATA lt_row_heights TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    DATA lt_col_widths  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY.

    DO lv_rows TIMES.
      APPEND get_splitter_row_height( lo_splitter ) TO lt_row_heights.
    ENDDO.

    DO lv_columns TIMES.
      APPEND get_splitter_col_width( lo_splitter ) TO lt_col_widths.
    ENDDO.

    APPEND VALUE ty_splitter_info( path          = i_path
                                   rows          = lv_rows
                                   columns       = lv_columns
                                   row_heights   = lt_row_heights
                                   column_widths = lt_col_widths )
           TO size_info.

    DATA(index_row) = 0.
    DATA(index_col) = 0.
    DO lv_rows TIMES.
      index_col = 0.
      index_row = index_row + 1.
      DO lv_columns TIMES.
        index_col = index_col + 1.
        DATA(lo_container) = lo_splitter->get_container( row    = index_row
                                                         column = index_col ).
        LOOP AT lo_container->children INTO DATA(lo_child).
          IF NOT analyze_recursive(
                     i_container = lo_child
                     i_path      = |{ i_path }[{ index_row },{ index_col }]| ).
            EXIT. " from do
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDDO.
    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->CONSTRUCTOR
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] REPID                          TYPE        CLIKE
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD constructor.
    me->repid = repid.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->DELETE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD delete.
    DATA(id) = get_id( ).
    DELETE FROM DATABASE indx(z1) ID id.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND size_info IS NOT INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'Splitter configuration deleted' TYPE 'S'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_COLUMN_COUNT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_SPLITTER                     TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_column_count.
    result = 0.
    DO 20 TIMES.
      DATA(container) = i_splitter->get_container( row    = 1
                                                   column = sy-index ).
      IF container IS INITIAL.
        RETURN.
      ELSE.
        result = result + 1.
      ENDIF.
    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_ID
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [<-()] R_ID                           TYPE        CHAR20
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_id.
    r_id = |SPLITTER-{ repid }-{ sy-uname }|.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_ROW_COUNT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_SPLITTER                     TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_row_count.
    result = 0.
    DO 20 TIMES.
      DATA(container) = i_splitter->get_container( row    = sy-index
                                                   column = 1 ).
      IF container IS INITIAL.
        RETURN.
      ELSE.
        result = result + 1.
      ENDIF.

    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_SPLITTER_COL_WIDTH
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] IO_SPLITTER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] R_SIZE                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_splitter_col_width.
    io_splitter->get_column_width( EXPORTING
                                     id     = sy-index
                                   IMPORTING
                                     result = r_size  ).
    cl_gui_cfw=>flush( ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_SPLITTER_ROW_HEIGHT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] IO_SPLITTER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] R_SIZE                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_splitter_row_height.
    io_splitter->get_row_height( EXPORTING
                                   id     = sy-index
                                 IMPORTING
                                   result = r_size  ).
    cl_gui_cfw=>flush( ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->LOAD
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD load.
    DATA(id) = get_id( ).
    IMPORT splitter_info TO size_info FROM DATABASE indx(z1) ID id.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND size_info IS NOT INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'Splitter configuration loaded' TYPE 'S'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->LOAD_AND_RESTORE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD load_and_restore.
    load( ).
    restore_sizes( i_container = i_container ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->RESTORE_RECURSIVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTROL
* | [--->] I_PATH                         TYPE        STRING
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD restore_recursive.
    TRY.
        DATA(splitter) = CAST cl_gui_splitter_container( i_container ) ##NO_TEXT.
      CATCH cx_sy_move_cast_error.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

    READ TABLE size_info INTO DATA(info) WITH KEY path = i_path.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR splitter IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    DATA(idx) = 1.

    LOOP AT info-row_heights INTO DATA(lv_r).
      splitter->set_row_height( id = idx height = lv_r ).
      idx = idx + 1.
    ENDLOOP.

    idx = 1.
    LOOP AT info-column_widths INTO DATA(lv_c).
      splitter->set_column_width( id = idx width = lv_c ).
      idx = idx + 1.
    ENDLOOP.

    " Rekursiv weiter
    DATA(index_row) = 0.
    DATA(index_col) = 0.
    DO info-rows TIMES.
      index_col = 0.
      index_row = index_row + 1.
      DO info-columns TIMES.
        index_col = index_col + 1.
        DATA(lo_container) = splitter->get_container( row    = index_row
                                                      column = index_col ).
        DATA(lv_subpath) = |{ i_path }[{ index_row },{ index_col }]|.
        LOOP AT lo_container->children INTO DATA(lo_child).
          restore_recursive( i_container = lo_child
                             i_path      = lv_subpath ).
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDDO.
    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->RESTORE_SIZES
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD restore_sizes.
    IF size_info IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT i_container->children INTO DATA(child).
      restore_recursive( i_container = child
                         i_path      = mc_root ).
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->SAVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD save.
    IF size_info IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    DATA(id) = get_id( ).
    EXPORT splitter_info FROM size_info TO DATABASE indx(z1) ID id.
    MESSAGE 'Splitter configuration saved' TYPE 'S'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Integrating with the Alerts System API in SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
@Ravisrp

as far as I am aware, there is no standard iFlow or BAdI available for this scenario. You would need to implement a custom solution. An iFlow is not strictly required—it could also be, for example, an SAP CAP service running on SAP BTP. The key requirement is that the resulting data structure matches the expected format.

For more details on the expected result structure, please refer to the SAP Help documentation:
https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SVC/56436b4e8fa84dc8b4408c7795a012c4/71260fe50b7b4114b5fd684471dc6ab5.html

entry.title: Re: Configuring OIDC between SAP IAS and S/4HANA (ABAP) in a RISE AWS Environment.
entry.content:
Thanks for the detailed steps on this!

Just to add some notes based on the experience configuring this:

Troubleshooting:

You can use in the ABAP side the report SEC_TRACE_ANALYZER. Also in IAS you can check in "troubleshooting Logs" and you'll find the details of the OIDC tokens generated and the attributes.

EntraID

If you have your IAS using EntraID as proxy, you'll be redirected to EntraID and same configuration works.

In case you want to configure EntraID directly in SOIDC you can use this issuer:

 https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant ID>/v2.0

In Entra you need to configure a new "app registration" but the steps are very simmilar to what you do in IAS

The possibility to use different claims have been introduced in newer versions of S/4HANA 2023 (SP2 or 3 I think) so in older versions integration with Entra was not possible due to the use of a specific attribute "sub" that Entra uses for different purpose than IAS.

STRUST

Make sure you have the corresponding CA certificates loaded in "SSL Client (Anonymous)" as trusted otherwise you'll get an error when loading the issuer in SOIDC

Thanks!

Diego

entry.title: Re: Partial Customer Project Invoice Request
entry.content:
Hi @KCPN ,

I do see couple of ways to do that.

Create Manual Billing Request via “Create Billing Request” App

If you don’t want to use invoice schedule, you can:
Go to Create Billing Request (Fiori app), select the customer project & WBS/item, choose the amount or percentage to bill

Use Billing Plan at Sales Order Level, If you are using Sales Integration.

If your customer project is tied to a Sales Order (SD), you can:
Create a Billing Plan in the sales order, subsequently, define percentage billing or milestones there
Use app - Manage Sales Orders

Partial Billing via API - This is Automated approach

If you’re looking for automation or system-based control:

Use API: A_ProjectBillingRequest or A_CustomerProject

Afterwards,build logic to:

a.Determine partial amounts
b.Trigger partial billing requests programmatically

I cannot think of any other possibiity apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Increase Alternative bank account number field S4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello @merle2,

Standard field lengths like the "Alternative Bank Account Number" are system-defined and cannot be extended via key user extensibility for sensitive objects like house bank master data in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

This limitation exists due to compliance and integration consistency across finance processes (e.g., bank statement processing, payment runs).

Options to consider:

  • Double-check the maximum allowed field length in the latest release notes or app documentation
  • Validate with your bank if a shorter account reference is acceptable for electronic statement uploads
  • If the limitation blocks critical processes, raising an SAP support incident is the recommended approach Enhancement requests for field extensions must go through the SAP Influence programs

Reference:

  • SAP Help – Maintain House Bank Accounts (S/4HANA Cloud)

Unfortunately, direct field length extension isn't possible in Public Cloud by design. Let me know if you want help drafting a support ticket.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Customer Project Grouping for reporting purposes
entry.content:
Hi @KCPN ,

Customer projects don’t support full-fledged “Project Hierarchies” like classic PS projects, you can still group them using available fields and tags that can then be used in project-related reporting.

A. Use Custom Fields (e.g., Grouping Tag or Category)
If standard fields are not sufficient, add a custom field (e.g., “Project Group”, “Region”, “Customer Segment”) using:

Within App: Custom Fields and Logic (F1481)

-Business Context: Project: Commercial Project
-Field Type: Code list (for group/category)
-Then enable the field in UI and CDS Views for reporting.

B. Use Work Package Tags / Naming Conventions

While Customer projects don’t support WBS hierarchies, you can:
Use structured naming for project IDs (e.g., REGION-CUST-001) and then add tags or indicators in work package names or descriptions

C. Create Custom CDS Views or Analytical Queries

If you need more advanced grouping/reporting:

Use Custom CDS Views (F1866A) to join:

-Customer project header
-Work packages
-Custom fields (e.g., grouping tags)
-Actual costs (from ACDOCA)

Build a Custom Analytical Query (Fiori app: “Custom Analytical Queries”)
Enable grouping in reporting by your custom dimension (e.g., Project Group)

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP CDC - Dataflow Reader Component: Delta Field for Conditional Updates
entry.content:
Hi Rvanjari,

Can you please open a support ticket so that we can see the exact details of the dataflow? Generally Shahar's suggestion is correct: if you run a dataflow once successfully in "pull all records" mode, subsequent delta runs should pick up all data. If there were no previous successful runs, it will take the last delta time.

Opening a ticket with specific details will let us examine your dataflow and see what went wrong.

Thanks!

entry.title: How to bind to an existing service without knowing its name (Cloud Foundry / SAP BTP)?

I’m deploying an app to multiple subaccounts in SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry), and I need to bind to an existing aicore service instance.The issue is that each subaccount already has an aicore service, but with different names (e.g. my-aicore, aicore-prod, etc).

If I use this in mta.yaml:
It creates a new service, which I want to avoid.

  - name: generative-ai-hub
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service-plan: extended
      service: aicore

If I try this:
It gives an error because there’s no service named generative-ai-hub in the subaccount.

  - name: generative-ai-hub
    type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
    parameters:
      service-plan: extended
      service: aicore

Is there a way to bind to an existing service without knowing its exact name?
Or maybe some way to reference it dynamically?

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud: App F0869A , message VL134 when sales B...
entry.content:
Hello @emmanuelm1 

Thanks for your message. The behaviour you’re seeing with error VL134 when using Sales BOMs with mixed EWM and IM stock is expected in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

The system does not allow splitting components of a Sales BOM across different warehouse types (EWM and IM) within the same delivery. This restriction prevents delivery creation when BOM components come from mixed sources.

 Ensure all BOM components are sourced from either EWM or IM locations only.  Alternatively, structure materials as separate sales items rather than within a single BOM

Check SAP documentation below

Outbound Deliveries – SAP Help

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Enabling email notification whenever a PLM ER workflow task is unlocked by admin using SWIA
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

During the PLM ER workflow execution process, if some one executes the workflow task through engineering inbox, then the "processor, Processed at, processed on" fields in ER for that task gets updated.

If some user executing the task and has not completed the task and went on vacation, the task gets locked with him. 

 In such case, only that user who executed the task can forward that to other user. If that user is out of office or in vacation , that task is stuck with him. 

As a result admin will unlock it by using the SWIA tcode in SAP PLM. 

Which means, workflow task status will be moved from "Executed" to "Work item Sent" status. 

Now in this case, when admin unlocks the task for that user , task will go back to all the users in the user group for that workflow task and they can act on it accordingly through "Engineering Inbox" . 

Whenever it is unlocked, it is required for the SAP system to auto generate an email to the Processor/User who is executed the task initially/ the user who is OOO. 

Email message 
Subject : Workflow task (ER# - Task Name) is un-reserved

Body: This is to inform you that the following Engineering Record (ER) Workflow task has been un-reserved:

  1. ER Name: 
  2. ER Type: 
  3. Task Name: 

to do above below config is required in SPRO- PLM WEB UI. (correct me if i am wrong)

Is it possible to add "Send e-mail when the task is unlocked" to the above ?

Where can i define these services in SAP PLM in-order to trigger email notification to process whenever the task is unlocked form the Processor/User who executed it. 

Please advice !

Best, 

Ramu Mannava

entry.title: Re: Sticky bit in Hana client
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, thank you for reporting this problem and this is a known issue. The SGID bit was added accidentally in builds 2.22.29 and 2.23.24 and higher.  This was fixed as issue #340453 in builds 2.24.24 and 2.25.22 and the SGID bit was removed again.

You can also remove the bit yourself manually without consequence: it is not required.

Best regards,
Jeff

entry.title: Notas Fiscais não processadas após execução do job EDOC_BR_DISTDFE_RETRIEVE

Gostaria de compartilhar uma situação que estamos enfrentando com o processo de distribuição de DF-e no SAP DRC, a fim de verificar se alguém já passou por algo semelhante ou pode oferecer alguma orientação.

Após a execução do job EDOC_BR_DISTDFE_RETRIEVE, observamos que algumas notas fiscais não são processadas corretamente e acabam sendo listadas no relatório de Documentos Pendentes (EDOC_BR_PENDING_DOCUMENT). No entanto, esses documentos não apresentam nenhum erro aparente e, ao reprocessá-los manualmente pela transação EDOC_BR_PENDING_DOC, eles são integrados com sucesso sem a necessidade de qualquer ajuste nos dados.

Alguém já enfrentou esse comportamento? Existe alguma recomendação de análise ou correção preventiva que possa ser aplicada nesse cenário?

Agradeço desde já pela colaboração!
